title: Session Mnemonics
text: Session Mnemonics These mnemonics start and end a session. Mnemonic Explanation init { ( Option ) } Initialize the device at the start of a session or when the display is refreshed. close Return the device to non-Uniface state. Arguments Table 1. Options Available with init and $DISPLAY_INIT Option Description 0 or setting not mentioned Causes all video attributes to be reinitialized at the end of a display. 1 Same as 0, but specifies the display as a stratus v102 display. 2 Same as 0, but specifies the display as an HP terminal. 3 Same as 0, but specifies the display as an HP terminal. 4 Allows video attributes to be initialized just before the next display, instead of at the end of the previous display. Also, the initialization will not occur when the cursor is already on the correct position for the next display and the attributes are equal to the incoming video attributes. This provides better performance than the options 1 through 3. Description The assignment setting $DISPLAY_INIT allows the init option to be changed without changing the entire device translation table. Example: Starting and ending a session BEGIN_DEVICE start device section ^esc= init select alternate keypad ^esc[1;24r close leave terminal in ’normal state’ ... ^esc[%v;%hH cursor(1,1) cursor posit command; home is 1,1 END_DEVICE end device section Copy Was this topic helpful?
